spec_match.rationale: No-developer-visible-change feature — by definition the API doesn't expose the new behaviour, so the demo can only probe indirectly. Honest about the limit but doesn't fully match the spec's framing.

interactive.rationale: 3 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects / 0 ranges driving live JS — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on data, each, probe, quota).

edge_cases.rationale: Page handles real failure modes — feature detection, permission rejection, fallback paths are all wired into the script.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/4977371751645184 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: incognito-detection probe (before-state) vs the new stable v
openQuestions.detail: The predictable-reported-storage-quota feature's headline framing — StorageManager.estimate() now reports a predictable quota that doesn't leak incognito-vs-regular state — calls out "incognito-detection probe (before-state) vs the new stable value". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: any genuine usage of quota planning
openQuestions.detail: The predictable-reported-storage-quota feature's headline framing — StorageManager.estimate() now reports a predictable quota that doesn't leak incognito-vs-regular state — calls out "any genuine usage of quota planning". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

summary: storage-breakdown under predictable-reported-storage-quota: 5 pass / 1 partial / 0 fail. Strong interactive surface, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
